description: Peclet number; measures plug-flow quality versus axial diffusion/dispersion. Higher Pe means less axial spreading and more plug-flow-like residence time distribution.
raw_calculation: Pe = 192 * tau_s * D_mol / d_m^2, as calculated by DesignCalculator.
notes: Pe >= 100 is treated as good plug-flow behavior; lower values indicate more axial dispersion.

description: Mass-transfer/mixing Damkohler-style indicator; measures whether mixing is slow relative to reaction/residence behavior. Lower Da_mass means less mixing limitation.
raw_calculation: Da_mass from DesignCalculator mass-transfer step, based on mixing time and reaction/residence scales.
good_direction: higher score is better after inversion
notes: The raw metric is lower-is-better, so the radar uses an inverted score.

description: Space-time yield; productivity normalized by reactor volume. Higher STY means more production per reactor volume per time.
normalization: score_STY_mol_L_h = minmax(log1p(STY_mol_L_h)) across all plotted pre/post cases
notes: Log scaling prevents very high productivity cases from dominating the radar.

description: Intensification factor versus batch residence/reaction time. Higher IF means the flow residence time is shorter relative to batch.
raw_calculation: IF = batch_time_min / tau_flow_min, reported by DesignCalculator.
notes: The score saturates at IF >= 6 so extreme IF values do not dominate.

description: Total heat-transfer conductance of the reactor. Higher UA means the reactor can exchange more heat per degree of temperature difference.
raw_calculation: UA = U_W_m2K * A_wall_m2, where A_wall = pi * d_m * L_m.
normalization: score_UA_W_K = minmax(log1p(UA_W_K)) across all plotted pre/post cases
notes: Uses U from DesignCalculator, typically 300 W/m2/K for coil in temperature-controlled bath. Raw UA is size-dependent.

description: Fraction of pump pressure capacity remaining after predicted reactor pressure drop. Higher headroom means safer and more practical operation.
notes: Uses dP_bar and pump_max_bar from DesignCalculator. A low score indicates risk of pump limitation or sensitivity to fouling/blockage.
